title: Rename process_data to transform_data for naming consistency
description: Renames the core processing method to align with the transform_ naming convention used across the service layer.
diff:
diff --git a/src/services/data_service.py b/src/services/data_service.py
index a1b2c3d..e4f5g6h 100644
--- a/src/services/data_service.py
+++ b/src/services/data_service.py
@@ -15,7 +15,7 @@ class DataService:
     def __init__(self, db: Database) -> None:
         self.db = db
 
-    def process_data(self, records: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    def transform_data(self, records: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
         """Apply transformations to incoming records before persisting."""
         result = []
         for record in records:
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ class DataService:
 
 def run_pipeline(service: DataService, raw_data: list[dict]) -> None:
     """Top-level ETL runner called by the scheduled job."""
-    transformed = service.process_data(raw_data)
+    transformed = service.transform_data(raw_data)
     service.db.bulk_insert(transformed)
diff --git a/tests/test_data_service.py b/tests/test_data_service.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/tests/test_data_service.py
+++ b/tests/test_data_service.py
@@ -18,5 +18,5 @@ def test_transform_records():
     service = DataService(db=FakeDB())
-    result = service.process_data([{"id": 1}, {"id": None}])
+    result = service.transform_data([{"id": 1}, {"id": None}])
     assert len(result) == 1
     assert result[0]["id"] == 1
human_notes: Pure rename; no logic changes. All call sites updated including tests. Briefing should describe the intent (naming consistency) not just 'renamed a method'. Questions should probe: are there other callers not shown in the diff? Is this a public API used by other services? Any serialized references to the old name?
